auth.title: Sign in to sync
auth.tagline: Keep your progress on every device
auth.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.

account.syncOfflineDetail: Offline — data will sync when you are back online
account.deleteWarning: Your account and all synced data will be permanently removed. Local progress will also be cleared. This cannot be undone.
account.deleteErrorRecentLogin: Sign out, sign in again, then try once more.
account.deleteErrorGeneric: Could not delete the account. Please try again.

grammar.resetBanner: Click the reset button again to confirm
grammar.alertNoRules: Mark at least one rule as learned first!

murphy.resetBanner: Click the reset button again to confirm
murphy.alertNoUnits: Mark at least one unit as learned first!

tensesTab.introRest: Answer the questions to get the right tense with a formula, examples, and common mistakes.

row.present_perfect: Past action with a present result
row.present_perfect_continuous: Started in the past and still continues
row.past_simple: Finished action at a specific past time
row.past_continuous: Longer background action in the past
row.past_perfect_continuous: Had been going on up to a past moment
row.future_will: Decision or prediction about the future
row.future_continuous: Action in progress at a future moment
row.future_perfect: Will be finished BEFORE a future point

present.q: What do you want to express?
present.c2: Link to the present: experience / result

present_now.q: Is it a permanent state or a process right now?

present_perfect_branch.q: Do you stress duration or the fact itself?

past_perfect_branch.q: Do you need to stress duration?
past_perfect_branch.c1: Yes — how long it had been going

future.q: How are you expressing the future?
future.c2: In progress at a future moment

present_simple.desc: Regular actions, habits, facts, and schedules.
present_simple.ex1: The sun rises in the east.
present_simple.ex1tr: The sun rises in the east.

present_continuous.desc: Action happening right now or a temporary situation. Also specific plans for the near future.
present_continuous.m0: <s>I am knowing</s> → stative verbs are not used in Continuous
present_continuous.m1: She is working usually → She usually works (habit = Present Simple)

past_simple.desc: A finished action in the past, with a specific time or an implied moment.
past_simple.m2: I have seen him yesterday → I saw him yesterday

past_continuous.desc: An action in progress at a past moment. Background action interrupted by another.
past_continuous.ex0: I was reading when she called.
past_continuous.ex0tr: I was reading when she called.
past_continuous.ex1: While he was cooking, she was working.
past_continuous.ex1tr: While he was cooking, she was working.

present_perfect.desc: A past action with a present result. Life experience. Unfinished period with for/since.
present_perfect.ex1: Have you ever been to Japan?
present_perfect.ex1tr: Have you ever been to Japan?
present_perfect.m0: I have seen him yesterday → I saw him yesterday
present_perfect.m2: I've lived here since five years → for five years

present_perfect_continuous.desc: Focus on duration or an unfinished process that started in the past.
present_perfect_continuous.ex0: I've been learning English for two years.
present_perfect_continuous.ex0tr: I've been learning English for two years.
present_perfect_continuous.ex1: Why are you tired? — I've been running.
present_perfect_continuous.ex1tr: Why are you tired? — I've been running.
present_perfect_continuous.m0: <s>I've been know</s> → stative verbs are not used in Continuous
present_perfect_continuous.m1: I've been learning since two years → for two years

past_perfect.desc: An action that finished before another moment in the past.
past_perfect.ex0: When I arrived, she had already left.
past_perfect.ex0tr: When I arrived, she had already left.
past_perfect.ex1: He had never seen snow before that winter.
past_perfect.ex1tr: He had never seen snow before that winter.
past_perfect.m0: When I arrived, she already left → she had already left
past_perfect.m1: He had never see → He had never seen

past_perfect_continuous.desc: How long something had been going on before a past moment. Explains cause or visible result.
past_perfect_continuous.ex0: She was exhausted — she'd been working all night.
past_perfect_continuous.ex0tr: She was exhausted — she'd been working all night.
past_perfect_continuous.ex1: How long had you been waiting?
past_perfect_continuous.ex1tr: How long had you been waiting?
past_perfect_continuous.m0: She had been work → She had been working
past_perfect_continuous.m1: How long had you waiting? → had you been waiting

future_will.desc: Predictions, spontaneous decisions, promises, general statements about the future.

future_going_to.desc: Planned intentions or predictions based on clear evidence.
future_going_to.ex1: Look at those clouds — it's going to rain!
future_going_to.ex1tr: Look at those clouds — it's going to rain!
future_going_to.m0: She is going to does → She is going to do
future_going_to.m1: <s>I am going to will do</s> → don't combine two future forms

future_continuous.desc: An action that will be in progress at a specific future time.
future_continuous.ex0: This time tomorrow I'll be lying on the beach.
future_continuous.ex0tr: This time tomorrow I'll be lying on the beach.
future_continuous.ex1: Don't call at 8 — I'll be having dinner.
future_continuous.ex1tr: Don't call at 8 — I'll be having dinner.

future_perfect.desc: An action that will be finished before a certain future moment.
future_perfect.ex1: By 2030 they will have built the bridge.
future_perfect.ex1tr: By 2030 they will have built the bridge.
future_perfect.m0: By Friday I will finish → I will have finished
